page: Amazon Web Services i7ie.2xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs derived from 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz. The processor includes a 320 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core.

For storage-heavy workloads, the instance includes 5000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. This high-capacity local storage is bundled directly with the instance, providing low-latency data access. Network connectivity is managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included with this instance type.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core capabilities, with the Geekbench single-core score and text processing performance ranking in the top 10%. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also show strong performance, placing in the top 25%. Memory performance is supported by DDR5 technology, with the Passmark memory score ranking in the top 25%. However, multi-core operations, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, align with average tier results. While small-model LLM text generation performs strongly, larger model text generation drops to the bottom 25% of tested systems, highlighting a performance tradeoff for larger AI workloads.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i7ie.2xlarge offers a high density of local NVMe SSD storage and DDR5 memory relative to its 8 vCPUs, making it a viable option for data-intensive tasks without the overhead of larger compute footprints. This hardware profile is suited for local database hosting, transactional databases, data indexing, text processing, and caching layers that require significant local storage and memory capacity.
description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.2xlarge is an x86_64 storage-optimized instance featuring an 8-vCPU Intel Xeon 8559C processor running at 3.6 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of 6 GHz DDR5 memory and 5000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core and text processing performance, alongside strong single-threaded compression and memory scores. However, multi-core benchmarks and larger LLM inference tasks fall into average and weak performance tiers, respectively. This resource configuration provides a high ratio of storage and memory relative to compute cores, offering qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive applications. The instance is suited for database hosting, text processing, and caching workloads requiring substantial local storage capacity.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.2xlarge is a storage-optimized instance with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and 5000 GB local NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core and text processing performance.

tagline: A storage-optimized instance combining 5000 GB of local NVMe SSD with DDR5 memory for data-intensive and text-processing workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8559C processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 320 MB L3 cache
64.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
5000 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core and text processing benchmark performance
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for database hosting, caching, and data-indexing workloads
